name: Magnetic Face
shortDescription: A magnetic drawing toy—drag the wand to sweep a heap of iron filings across a cartoon face and give him a wild hairdo, a bushy beard, thick eyebrows, and a moustache. The filings comb into the magnetic field just like the real thing. Shake it off to start over.

name: Gravity Orbits
shortDescription: A little gravity sandbox—fling planets around a glowing star and watch them fall into orbit, trace glowing elliptical trails, slingshot past each other, and merge into bigger worlds. Build your own tiny solar system.

name: Cloth & Tear
shortDescription: A hanging piece of cloth simulated with real verlet physics—drag to swing and stretch the fabric, then yank hard or swipe fast to tear holes right through it. Flip on a breeze to watch it ripple. Soft, tactile, endlessly fiddleable.

name: Double Pendulum
shortDescription: A chaotic double pendulum with faithful physics—drag an arm and let go to send it whipping into wild, never-repeating motion that paints glowing rainbow trails across the dark. A hands-on chaos-theory sandbox.

name: Aurebesh Translator
shortDescription: Type a message in English and watch it transliterate live into Aurebesh—the writing system of the Star Wars galaxy—on a glowing Imperial datapad, glyph by glyph. Hover any character to learn its letter and name, flip on the captions to read along, and copy a link that opens your secret message already decoded. Soft terminal bleeps as you type.

name: Pottery Wheel
shortDescription: A calming digital pottery wheel—a lump of clay spins on the wheel and you drag up and down its side to pull the walls out and in, shaping a vase, bowl, or pot in soft 3D. Then fire it in the kiln to reveal your finished, glazed piece. Soft wheel hum and wet-clay shaping sounds.

name: Chladni Plate
shortDescription: A digital cymatics sand plate—thousands of grains shake across a vibrating square and settle onto the nodal lines, drawing intricate standing-wave figures. Press and drag to pour sand anywhere on the plate, change the frequency faster or slower, and pause to watch it settle.

name: Pin Art
shortDescription: A fullscreen digital metal pinscreen—the relief rises under your hand, and you can press and drag to draw a lasting 3D impression in glowing metal pins. Erase or clear to start fresh.

name: Perfect Circle
shortDescription: Draw a circle freehand in one stroke and get scored on how perfectly round it is. A color heat-map shows where you wandered—chase 100%, beat your best, and try just one more time.

name: Rain on a Window
shortDescription: A cozy rainy night through a foggy pane—drag to wipe the glass and reveal warm bokeh city lights, while raindrops cling, grow, and slide down in clear trails. Ambient rain and distant thunder.

name: Fireworks
shortDescription: Tap the night sky to launch fireworks—shells arc up and burst into glowing, crackling sparks that drift down and reflect on the water below, with layered boom-and-crackle sound.

name: Campfire
shortDescription: A crackling fire on a dark night—tap to toss on a log, drag to fan the flames brighter. Embers drift up, the glow warms, and it crackles with fully synthesized fire sound.

name: Aurora Drift
shortDescription: Drag across a starlit sky to paint slow, shimmering curtains of northern lights over snowy peaks—green and violet ribbons that sway and softly fade.

name: Floating Lanterns
shortDescription: A calm night lake under a full moon—tap the water to release a glowing paper lantern that drifts up into the stars, swaying and flickering, mirrored on the surface below.

name: Zen Sand Garden
shortDescription: A miniature Japanese rock garden—drag to rake calm flowing lines through the sand, tap to set a smooth stone, and watch the furrows ripple out around it.

name: Marble Drop
shortDescription: Drop glossy glass marbles onto a brass pegboard and watch them bounce, clack, and pile up in the bins below. Tap anywhere up top to drop one.

name: Wind Chimes
shortDescription: A set of hanging metal wind chimes—drag to send a breeze through them or tap a tube to ring it, and hear calm, consonant tones float on the warm evening air.

name: Zen Ripple Pond
shortDescription: A still moonlit pond—tap or drag the water and watch real ripples spread, collide, and shimmer across the surface while koi drift below the lily pads.

name: Kaleidoscope
shortDescription: A living kaleidoscope—drag to stir glowing jewel shards into endless mirrored symmetry, and tap to drop a new gem. Hypnotic and ever-shifting.

name: Plasma Ball
shortDescription: A glowing plasma ball—electric arcs branch and crackle from the central electrode, and chase your finger when you touch the glass.

name: Spirograph
shortDescription: A self-drawing digital spirograph—watch geometric petal patterns bloom and shift through color as each curve completes and the next begins. Tap to skip.

name: Newton's Cradle
shortDescription: A satisfying Newton's Cradle simulation—swing 1, 2, or 3 balls and watch momentum transfer in perfect clacking loops. Mesmerizing physics.

name: Bubble Wrap
shortDescription: A full sheet of virtual bubble wrap—tap or drag to pop satisfying bubbles with a crisp audio crack. Reset for a fresh sheet. Endlessly satisfying.

name: Lava Lamp
shortDescription: A full-screen digital lava lamp—warm amber blobs slowly rise, merge, and drift apart in hypnotic metaball loops. Just watch.

name: Falling Sand
shortDescription: A material sandbox—draw with sand, water, fire, plant, oil, and stone and watch them fall, flow, burn, and grow. Water douses fire, sand sinks, fire eats plants.

name: Beat Maker
shortDescription: A glowing step sequencer—tap the grid to build a looping beat with drums and a pentatonic melody as the playhead sweeps. Tuned so every pattern sounds good.

name: Chimp Test
shortDescription: The famous working-memory challenge—numbers flash on a grid then hide; click them in order from memory, one more each round. Can you beat a chimp?

name: Echo
shortDescription: A glowing Simon-style memory game—watch the growing pattern of light and sound, then echo it back. How long a sequence can you hold?

name: Tiny Idle Garden
shortDescription: Plant seeds and watch flowers grow and bloom in real time under a drifting day–night sky—it keeps growing while you're away.

name: Star Click Sky
shortDescription: Click to burst stars into a drifting cosmos of nebulae and shooting stars—your stars connect into glowing constellations.

name: Tic-Tac-Toe
shortDescription: Classic noughts and crosses vs the computer—Chill mode is winnable, Ruthless is an unbeatable minimax AI. Neon board, instant rematch.

name: Rock Paper Scissors
shortDescription: Throw rock, paper, or scissors against the computer, watch the clash, and chase a winning streak.

name: Three Doors
shortDescription: The Monty Hall problem as a game show—pick a door, the host reveals a goat, then switch or stay. Watch the 2/3 vs 1/3 win rates emerge.

name: Memory Match
shortDescription: Flip cards two at a time and find every pair from memory. Three grid sizes, a move counter, and a timer.

name: Snake
shortDescription: Classic neon snake—eat, grow, and chase a high score with arrow keys, WASD, swipe, or the on-screen pad. Best score saved locally.

name: Weird Generative Canvas
shortDescription: A generative-art playground: flow fields, harmonographs, swarms, and kaleidoscopes. Switch modes and palettes, drag to steer, and save a PNG.

name: Dice Roller
shortDescription: Roll faceted 3D dice—d4 through d20, solo or a whole pool at once. They tumble and settle fair, with a running total and history.

name: Magic 8-Ball
shortDescription: Ask a yes/no question and shake the glossy 8-ball—the window clouds over and the blue triangle surfaces with your answer.

name: Blob Choir
shortDescription: A cluster of glowing gooey blobs that sing warm synth voices. Tap or drag to play; hit Shimmer for generative ambient.

name: Coin Flip
shortDescription: Toss a real 3D gold coin—it arcs, tumbles, and lands heads or tails with cryptographically fair odds. Tracks streaks and your run.

name: Breathing Pacer
shortDescription: A calm glowing orb that expands and contracts to pace your breath—Calm, Box, or 4·7·8, with an optional ambient tone.

name: Meeting Cost Meter
shortDescription: Set the hourly rate and headcount, hit start, and watch a live dollar counter climb as the meeting drags on.

name: Goo Cursor
shortDescription: Chrome-and-lava metaballs that trail your cursor and merge like living ferrofluid. Tap to splat.
